subject: Validate e2m model.ts <-> tables/*.sql parity + semver bump policy

pass_if: every zod entity in model.ts (Agent, Team, TeamMember, Envelope, DurableTask, Transition) maps 1:1 to a tables/*.sql file with matching columns, types, and enum members
each enum in model.ts (TaskState, TransitionEvent, EnvelopeType, EnvelopeState, AgentFunction, AgentTier) equals the corresponding postgres CREATE TYPE ... AS ENUM members exactly
EM_MODEL_VERSION and every table semantics 'version:' header agree, and the @semver bump policy (MAJOR breaking / MINOR additive / PATCH docs) is asserted by a check
fail_if: any column, type, FK, or enum member exists in one side but not the other
a table semantics version drifts from EM_MODEL_VERSION without a documented bump

subject: Add v_task_completion VIEW: completion % per queue from durable_task.state

pass_if: tables/v_task_completion.sql defines a VIEW grouping durable_task by queue with total_count, completed_count, and completion_pct = completed_count::numeric / nullif(total_count,0)
completed_count counts state='completed'; completion_pct is null-safe for empty queues (no divide-by-zero)
the view file carries a >>> semantics ... <<< header (entity, version, grain=one row per queue, owner=data-manager, source) consistent with durable_task.sql
fail_if: division by zero on an empty queue
completion_pct not bounded to [0,1] or miscounts non-completed states

subject: Add v_agent_throughput VIEW joining transition + agent

pass_if: tables/v_agent_throughput.sql defines a VIEW joining transition.owner -> agent.id, grouped per agent with completed_count (event='complete') and a throughput measure over time
grain and join keys honor transition PK (id, at) and agent.id; fn and tier from agent are exposed as dimensions
the view file carries a >>> semantics ... <<< header (entity, version, grain=one row per agent, owner=data-manager, relationships to transition + agent)
fail_if: join uses a non-existent column or wrong FK direction
throughput double-counts retried transitions or ignores the 'complete' event filter
